text: We present a case of emergency laparoscopy heartman procedure in an eighteen years old woman with history of morbid obesity, rheumatoid arthritis, hypertension and previous hysterectomy. Patient presented during the afternoon with symptoms of acute peritonitis on examination and fever. Abdomen CT scan with contrast shows three perforated diverticulitis of the sigmoid colon. In the last update of the American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons guidelines on the treatment of left side colonic diverticulitis, four-inch T-to-four laparoscopic lavage is not recommended. And following collect me The decision to restore bowel continuity should incorporate patient factors, interpretive factors, and surgeon preference. Despite recommendations regarding the outcomes of primary anastomosis after colectomy for ING-G to IV, adoption rates of this approach remain low.

The surgeon incises the peritoneum and identifies the sigmoidal vessels. These vessels are isolated and sectioned between hemlock clips, amidst the visible signs of fecal peritonitis in the abdominal cavity.

Laparoscopy heartman procedure should make the subsequent reversal by laparoscopy more technically feasible so as to reduce its morbidity. Compared with primary anastomosis, laparoscopic heart mount procedure forecast needs the most complex part of the surgery, takedown of the splenic fracture and anastomosis, from emergency to elective surgery. All proven benefits of elective laparoscopic colectomy on abdominal wall complications should be equivalent in an emergency setting.

The surgeon uses high-energy tools to open the left paracolic gutter, freeing the descending colon for colostomy. The specimen is insulated in an iron bag to prevent fecal contamination, followed by a thorough wash of the abdominal cavity.

There was no intraoperative blood loss. Their overall surgical time was sixty-five minutes. Resumption of solid foods was on the two GH postoperative day and discourage on the sixty postoperative day.
